rationale: The cited paper is used as the source of a specific hedonic-value measurement scale, not just as general background. The citing paper explicitly applies Dhar and Wertenbroch's seven-point hedonic scale in follow-up analyses (for products in Studies 2 and 4, and an additional yogurt-ketchup check) to examine whether hedonic appeal could explain its stockpiling results. Those scale-based checks are then used to interpret findings as convenience-driven rather than hedonicity-driven. This is a concrete methodological use of the cited work.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Dhar and Wertenbroch's hedonic-utilitarian scale to measure product hedonic value in supplementary analyses. It uses those measurements to rule out hedonic-value confounds when interpreting the stockpiling and convenience effects.
